title: Iranian President’s Apology to Neighboring Gulf States Showcases Leadership Rifts
summary: Masoud Pezeshkian, the Iranian president, apologized for Iranian strikes on Gulf states before backtracking after criticism from other Iranian leaders. Despite his remarks, Iran has continued its attacks.

title: Gas Prices Continue to Surge in U.S., Rising 14% in a Week
summary: Soaring oil prices suggest that more increases could be in store for American drivers. Diesel, jet fuel, and other refined products are also becoming much more expensive.

title: How We Analyzed the Strike on the Iranian School
summary: Malachy Browne of our Visual Investigations team describes what satellite imagery and other evidence tell us about who might be responsible for an airstrike on an elementary school in southern Iran. The strike killed at least 175 people, according to health officials and Iranian state media.

title: Kristi Noem Survived Many Crises. Then She Crossed a Trump Red Line.
summary: President Trump, who values loyalty, has at times tried to distance himself from his administration’s own actions when they become politically toxic.

title: At Trump’s Summit in Miami, Bolivia Makes a Political U-turn Toward the U.S.
summary: For the past two decades, Bolivia resisted U.S. influence. A rightward shift is reorienting the country’s president toward Washington.

title: ICE Detains Reporter Estefany Rodriguez Florez in Nashville
summary: The detention of Estefany Maria Rodriguez Florez, who had sought asylum and is married to an American citizen, raised fears that she had been targeted for her reporting.

title: At Noma, Accusations of Physical Abuse
summary: Dozens of former employees say René Redzepi inflicted physical and psychological violence on the staff for years.

title: For OpenAI and Anthropic, the Competition Is Deeply Personal
summary: A fight over Pentagon contracts shows how the leaders of Silicon Valley’s two most important A.I. start-ups are feuding over the future of the tech industry.

title: Anthropic’s and OpenAI’s Dance With the Pentagon: What to Know
summary: Negotiations, threats and amended contracts have left plenty of questions. Here are some answers.

title: How Candidates Are Using Winks and Posts to Seek Crypto and A.I. Cash
summary: All across America, congressional candidates are finding creative — and critics say cynical — ways to signal support for two deep-pocketed industries, A.I. and crypto.

title: How Talarico Won Texas Democrats With Love, Luck and a Little Restraint
summary: A carefully disciplined campaign that capitalized on viral media, months of organizing and strong outreach to Latino voters helped propel James Talarico to the center of Texas politics.

title: Ahead of Midterms, Economic Warning Signs Pile Up for Republicans
summary: With employers cutting jobs and gas prices rising amid the war in Iran, Democrats see an opportunity to press their advantage.

title: Brandon Herrera’s YouTube Gives Democrats More Hope in West Texas Race
summary: What had been a safe G.O.P. seat was looking more attainable for Democrats after Representative Tony Gonzales bowed out in favor of a hard-right candidate.

title: How Alberto Carvalho Became L.A.U.S.D. Superintendent Despite Scandals
summary: Alberto Carvalho was seen as a catch for the nation’s second largest school district. Then his home and office were raided by the F.B.I.

title: ‘Wuthering Heights,’ MAGA Style
summary: Kristi and Corey cavorted in the swamp as D.H.S. got bogged down.

title: There Is One Crucial Reason We’re Talking About Boots on the Ground
summary: The U.S. and Israel have no good options to address an unavoidable problem.

title: Hanging Up
summary: If decreasing dependence on our phones feels impossible, we might benefit from considering what we’d want to fill the space that they occupy.

title: Epstein Doctor Steps Away From Elite Health Clinics
summary: Dr. Bernard Kruger once gave a private E.R. membership to Jeffrey Epstein and unnamed “girls.”

title: Russia Attacks Kharkiv and Kyiv in Ukraine
summary: At least 10 people were killed when a Russian missile hit a five-story apartment building in the city of Kharkiv, and Kyiv and several other regions also came under attack.

title: A Fight Is Brewing Over Building Housing in Place of a NoHo Parking Lot
summary: Some residents of the historic Manhattan neighborhood oppose the construction of two apartment towers.
